raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Assembly Bill 61 would empower local jurisdictions and the Department of Alcohol Beverage Control to provide regulatory flexibility to support struggling restaurants.",
    "Outdoor dining offers a lifeline that can help keep neighborhood restaurants afloat during challenging times.",
    "The restaurant industry has faced significant challenges due to the pandemic, particularly with indoor dining rooms being closed in most places.",
    "Outdoor dining is likely to be critical for many restaurants for the foreseeable future despite progress with vaccines.",
    "AB 61 would temporarily waive requirements for fully enclosed kitchen and service areas to allow for increased airflow and new take-out windows.",
    "AB 61 would allow restaurants to utilize their parking spaces for increased outdoor dining capacity.",
    "AB 61 would enable business activity districts to lower speed limits in certain areas to enhance outdoor dining safety.",
    "AB 61 would temporarily extend existing regulatory relief from the Department of Alcohol Beverage Control, allowing for expanded outdoor dining and outdoor alcohol-to-go sales.",
    "AB 61 would grant the Alcohol Beverage Control flexibility to allow temporary changes to existing alcohol licenses.",
    "AB 61 would permit the Alcohol Beverage Control to develop a licensing process to regulate third-party delivery of alcohol to prevent access by minors.",
    "AB 61 would allow coffee bars to be set up and operate outdoors.",
    "California restaurants estimate they have lost $6.2 billion in sales since April 2020 due to the pandemic.",
    "79% of restaurant owners in California reported that it is unlikely their restaurants will be profitable in the next six months.",
    "Over a million employees in the California restaurant industry have been furloughed or laid off.",
    "The restaurant industry in California employs one of the most diverse workforces in the state, with six out of ten restaurants owned by people of color."
  ]
}
```
